title: 西塞山怀古
titleEn: Mount Xisai: Thoughts on the Past
author: 刘禹锡
content:
王濬楼船下益州
金陵王气黯然收
千寻铁锁沉江底
一片降幡出石头
人世几回伤往事
山形依旧枕寒流
今逢四海为家日
故垒萧萧芦荻秋
translation:
Wang Jun's tower ships descend from Yi Zhou
Jinling's royal aura darkens and fades
A thousand fathoms of iron chain sink to the river bottom
One surrender banner rises from Stone City
How many times has the world grieved over the past?
The mountain's shape remains, pillowing the cold stream
Now, when the four seas are one home
The old fortress, desolate, reeds and rushes in autumn
annotation: An empire falls, iron chains sink, a banner rises. But the mountain remains. Liu Yuxi sees history's pattern: everything changes, the land stays.

title: 石头城
author: 刘禹锡
content:
山围故国周遭在
潮打空城寂寞回
淮水东边旧时月
夜深还过女墙来
translation:
Mountains surround the old kingdom, still all around
Waves hit the empty city, lonely, they return
The old moon east of the Huai River
Late at night, still passes over the parapet wall
annotation: The city is empty, the mountains remain. The moon that shone on the glorious past still rises. Liu Yuxi finds the moon to be the truest historian.

title: 乌衣巷
author: 刘禹锡
content:
朱雀桥边野草花
乌衣巷口夕阳斜
旧时王谢堂前燕
飞入寻常百姓家
translation:
By Rosefinch Bridge, wild grass and flowers
At Black Clothes Lane, the setting sun slants
The swallows that once nested in the halls of Wang and Xie
Now fly into the homes of ordinary people
annotation: Great families fell, their mansions crumbled. But the swallows still return — they just nest in common homes now. A poem about how time humbles everyone.

title: 望洞庭
author: 刘禹锡
content:
湖光秋月两相和
潭面无风镜未磨
遥望洞庭山水翠
白银盘里一青螺
translation:
Lake light and autumn moon harmonize
The pool's surface, no wind, a mirror unpolished
From afar, Dongting's mountains and water, emerald
A green snail in a silver plate
annotation: Dongting Lake at night: the moon and water become one. From above, the lake is a silver plate with a green island like a snail. Liu Yuxi's metaphor is unforgettable.

title: 浪淘沙
author: 刘禹锡
content:
九曲黄河万里沙
浪淘风簸自天涯
如今直上银河去
同到牵牛织女家
translation:
Nine bends of the Yellow River, ten thousand miles of sand
Waves wash, wind shakes, from the edge of the sky
Now I'll go straight up to the Milky Way
And visit the Cowherd and Weaver Girl together
annotation: The Yellow River's sand comes from the edge of the sky. Liu Yuxi imagines riding the waves all the way to heaven to meet the legendary lovers. Even dust can reach the stars.

title: 秋词
author: 刘禹锡
content:
自古逢秋悲寂寥
我言秋日胜春朝
晴空一鹤排云上
便引诗情到碧霄
translation:
Since ancient times, autumn brings sorrow and loneliness
I say autumn days surpass spring mornings
In the clear sky, a crane parts the clouds
And leads my poetic feelings to the blue heavens
annotation: Everyone says autumn is sad. Liu Yuxi disagrees. A crane soaring through clouds — autumn is more inspiring than spring! This is optimism as rebellion.

title: 酬乐天扬州初逢席上见赠
titlePinyin: Chou Le Tian Yang Zhou Chu Feng Xi Shang Jian Zeng
titleEn: Reply to Bai Juyi at Our First Meeting in Yangzhou
author: 刘禹锡
content:
巴山楚水凄凉地
二十三年弃置身
怀旧空吟闻笛赋
到乡翻似烂柯人
沉舟侧畔千帆过
病树前头万木春
今日听君歌一曲
暂凭杯酒长精神
translation:
The bleak lands of Ba and Chu
Twenty-three years, I was cast aside
Remembering the past, I vainly recite the flute poem
Returning home, I'm like the man with the rotten axe handle
A thousand sails pass by the sunken boat
Ten thousand trees bloom before the sick tree
Today I hear you sing one song
For now, I'll borrow wine to lift my spirit
annotation: After 23 years of exile, Liu Yuxi returns. 'A thousand sails pass the sunken boat' — the world moved on without him. But he's not bitter: 'Ten thousand trees bloom before the sick tree.' Life goes on.

title: 卖炭翁
author: 白居易
content:
卖炭翁
伐薪烧炭南山中
满面尘灰烟火色
两鬓苍苍十指黑
卖炭得钱何所营
身上衣裳口中食
可怜身上衣正单
心忧炭贱愿天寒
夜来城外一尺雪
晓驾炭车辗冰辙
牛困人饥日已高
市南门外泥中歇
翩翩两骑来是谁
黄衣使者白衫儿
手把文书口称敕
回车叱牛牵向北
一车炭
千余斤
宫使驱将惜不得
半匹红纱一丈绫
系向牛头充炭直
translation:
A charcoal seller
Cuts wood and burns charcoal in the Southern Mountain
Dust and smoke color his face
Temples gray, ten fingers black
What does he spend his charcoal money on?
Clothes for his body, food for his mouth
Pitiful — his clothes are thin
But he worries charcoal is cheap and wishes for cold weather
Last night, a foot of snow outside the wall
At dawn, he drives his charcoal cart over ice ruts
The cow is tired, the man hungry, the sun already high
He rests in the mud outside the south market gate
Two riders come flying — who are they?
Yellow-robed messenger, white-clad boy
Holding documents, mouth declaring imperial orders
They turn the cart, shout at the cow, pull it north
One cart of charcoal
Over a thousand pounds
The palace envoy drives it away — he can't stop them
Half a bolt of red silk, a length of gauze
Tied to the cow's head — the price of charcoal
annotation: An old man makes charcoal in the snow. The palace takes it all, paying with worthless silk. Bai Juyi's most direct protest poem — the system grinds down the poor.

title: 长相思
author: 白居易
content:
汴水流
泗水流
流到瓜洲古渡头
吴山点点愁
思悠悠
恨悠悠
恨到归时方始休
月明人倚楼
translation:
The Bian River flows
The Si River flows
Flowing to the ancient crossing at Guazhou
Wu's mountains, dot by dot, sorrow
Thinking, endless
Hating, endless
The hate only stops when you return
Bright moon, a person leans on the tower
annotation: Rivers flow toward the one she misses. Mountains look like dots of sorrow. The hate (of separation) only ends when he returns. Bai Juyi makes geography into emotion.

title: 离思
author: 元稹
content:
曾经沧海难为水
除却巫山不是云
取次花丛懒回顾
半缘修道半缘君
translation:
Having seen the vast sea, no water satisfies
Having seen Wu Mountain's clouds, no other clouds count
Passing through flower gardens, I'm too lazy to look back
Half because of my spiritual path, half because of you
annotation: After the greatest love, nothing else compares. 'Having seen the sea, no other water satisfies.' Yuan Zhen wrote this for his late wife. It's become the ultimate love declaration.

title: 行宫
author: 元稹
content:
寥落古行宫
宫花寂寞红
白头宫女在
闲坐说玄宗
translation:
Desolate, the ancient traveling palace
Palace flowers, lonely, red
White-haired palace women sit
Idly talking about Emperor Xuanzong
annotation: Four lines, an entire empire's story. The palace is empty, the flowers bloom for no one, old women gossip about the glorious past. Yuan Zhen captures the fall of an empire in 20 characters.

title: 游子吟
author: 孟郊
content:
慈母手中线
游子身上衣
临行密密缝
意恐迟迟归
谁言寸草心
报得三春晖
translation:
In a loving mother's hands, thread
On her traveling son's body, clothes
Before departure, she sews stitch by stitch
Fearing his return will be delayed
Who says the heart of grass
Can repay the spring sunshine?
annotation: A son's gratitude to his mother. The final metaphor is unforgettable: a tiny grass can never repay the warmth of the spring sun, just as children can never fully repay their parents' love.

title: 登科后
author: 孟郊
content:
昔日龌龊不足夸
今朝放荡思无涯
春风得意马蹄疾
一日看尽长安花
translation:
The past's frustrations aren't worth boasting about
Today, my thoughts roam without bounds
In spring wind, proud, my horse's hooves swift
In one day, I see all of Chang'an's flowers
annotation: After years of failure, Meng Jiao finally passes the exam at age 46. 'In one day, I see all of Chang'an's flowers.' This is what victory feels like after a lifetime of struggle.

title: 古怨别
author: 孟郊
content:
飒飒秋风生
愁人怨离别
含情两相向
欲语气先咽
心曲千万端
悲来却难说
别后唯所思
天涯共明月
translation:
Rustling, rustling, autumn wind rises
The sorrowful resent parting
Full of feeling, they face each other
About to speak, the voice chokes first
A heart's ten thousand curves
When sorrow comes, it's hard to speak
After parting, only thoughts of each other
At the edge of the sky, sharing the bright moon
annotation: They want to speak but can't. The words choke. After parting, only the moon connects them. Meng Jiao knows: the deepest feelings are the hardest to say.

title: 题李凝幽居
author: 贾岛
content:
闲居少邻并
草径入荒园
鸟宿池边树
僧敲月下门
过桥分野色
移石动云根
暂去还来此
幽期不负言
translation:
Leisurely dwelling, few neighbors
A grass path enters the wild garden
Birds sleep in the tree by the pool
A monk knocks on the gate under the moon
Crossing the bridge, the field's colors part
Moving stones, the cloud's roots shift
I'll leave for now but return
This secluded promise won't be broken
annotation: The famous 'knock' vs 'push' story. Jia Dao couldn't decide: should the monk knock or push? He met Han Yu on the street, who said 'knock' — more polite, more poetic. This is the origin of 'polishing' (推敲) in Chinese.

title: 剑客
author: 贾岛
content:
十年磨一剑
霜刃未曾试
今日把示君
谁有不平事
translation:
Ten years, I've sharpened one sword
Its frost blade has never been tested
Today I show it to you
Who has an injustice to right?
annotation: A swordsman who's spent ten years preparing. Today he's ready. 'Who has an injustice?' It's a job application — but with a sword. Jia Dao's ambition, sharpened to a point.

title: 不第后赋菊
titleEn: After Failing the Exam: Ode to Chrysanthemums
author: 黄巢
content:
待到秋来九月八
我花开后百花杀
冲天香阵透长安
满城尽带黄金甲
translation:
When autumn comes, the eighth of the ninth month
After my flowers bloom, all other flowers die
A fragrant array charges through Chang'an
The whole city wears golden armor
annotation: Failed the exam, so he wrote about chrysanthemums as revolution. 'The whole city wears golden armor' — this is a threat disguised as a flower poem. Huang Chao later led a massive rebellion.

title: 春怨
author: 金昌绪
content:
打起黄莺儿
莫教枝上啼
啼时惊妾梦
不得到辽西
translation:
Drive away the yellow oriole
Don't let it sing on the branch
Its singing startles my dream
And I can't reach Liaoxi
annotation: A woman shoos away a singing bird because it interrupts her dream of her husband at the frontier. The smallest annoyance becomes the greatest grief.

title: 哥舒歌
author: 西鄙人
content:
北斗七星高
哥舒带夜刀
至今窥牧马
不敢过临洮
translation:
The Big Dipper's seven stars high
Geshu carries his sword at night
To this day, watching their herding horses
They dare not cross Lintao
annotation: A folk song about General Geshu Han. Just his name and his sword keep the barbarians away. The people sing: as long as he's there, we're safe.

title: 陇西行
author: 陈陶
content:
誓扫匈奴不顾身
五千貂锦丧胡尘
可怜无定河边骨
犹是春闺梦里人
translation:
Sworn to sweep away the Xiongnu, regardless of life
Five thousand sable and brocade, lost in barbarian dust
Pitiful — the bones by the Wuding River
Are still the person in a spring chamber's dream
annotation: Five thousand soldiers died. Their bones are by the river. But their wives still dream of them coming home. The most devastating war poem — the dead are still alive in someone's dreams.

title: 春江花月夜
author: 张若虚
content:
春江潮水连海平
海上明月共潮生
滟滟随波千万里
何处春江无月明
江流宛转绕芳甸
月照花林皆似霰
空里流霜不觉飞
汀上白沙看不见
江天一色无纤尘
皎皎空中孤月轮
江畔何人初见月
江月何年初照人
人生代代无穷已
江月年年望相似
不知江月待何人
但见长江送流水
translation:
Spring river tide meets the sea, level
On the sea, the bright moon rises with the tide
Sparkling, following the waves, ten thousand miles
Where is the spring river without moonlight?
The river winds around fragrant meadows
The moon shines on flower forests like snow
In the sky, flowing frost, you don't feel it flying
On the islet, white sand, you can't see it
River and sky, one color, no dust
Bright, bright, the lone moon in the sky
By the river, who first saw the moon?
The river's moon — what year did it first shine on people?
Life after life, endless
The river moon, year after year, looks the same
I don't know who the river moon waits for
I only see the long river sending flowing water
annotation: The greatest moon poem in Chinese. Zhang Ruoxu asks: who first saw the moon? When did the moon first see us? The river flows, the moon stays, people come and go. It's philosophy dressed as a landscape painting.

title: 望月怀远
titleEn: Gazing at the Moon, Thinking of the Distant
author: 张九龄
content:
海上生明月
天涯共此时
情人怨遥夜
竟夕起相思
灭烛怜光满
披衣觉露滋
不堪盈手赠
还寝梦佳期
translation:
Over the sea, the bright moon rises
At the edge of the sky, we share this moment
Lovers resent the distant night
All night, longing arises
I extinguish the candle, loving the full light
Put on clothes, feel the dew forming
I can't hold it all in my hand to give you
I'll return to bed and dream of a good meeting
annotation: The moon rises over the sea. Far away, you see it too. Zhang Jiuling can't send the moon to his loved one, so he'll dream of them instead. The most tender moon poem.

title: 感遇
author: 张九龄
content:
兰叶春葳蕤
桂华秋皎洁
欣欣此生意
自尔为佳节
谁知林栖者
闻风坐相悦
草木有本心
何求美人折
translation:
Orchid leaves in spring, lush
Cassia flowers in autumn, bright and clean
Joyfully, this vitality
Makes its own beautiful season
Who knows the forest dweller
Hears the wind and sits in delight
Grass and trees have their own hearts
Why seek a beauty's picking?
annotation: Orchids bloom in spring, cassia in autumn — each in its season. They don't need anyone to pick them. Zhang Jiuling's message: be yourself, for yourself.

title: 下终南山过斛斯山人宿置酒
titlePinyin: Xia Zhong Nan Shan Guo Hu Si Shan Ren Su Zhi Jiu
titleEn: Descending Zhongnan Mountain, Staying with Hermit Hu Si
author: 李白
content:
暮从碧山下
山月随人归
却顾所来径
苍苍横翠微
相携及田家
童稚开荆扉
绿竹入幽涧
青萝拂行衣
欢言得所憩
美酒聊共挥
长歌吟松风
曲尽河星稀
我醉君复乐
陶然共忘机
translation:
At dusk I descend the green mountain
The mountain moon follows me home
Looking back at the path I came
Blue-green, crossing the emerald haze
We walk together to the farm
Children open the brush gate
Green bamboo enters the quiet ravine
Blue vines brush our traveling clothes
Happy talk, a good rest
Fine wine, we wave it freely
Long songs, singing with the pine wind
When the song ends, the river stars are thin
I'm drunk, you're happy
Together, we forget all schemes
annotation: Li Bai descends the mountain with the moon as his companion. A hermit's family welcomes him. Wine, songs, stars — and the best line: 'Together, we forget all schemes.' This is what friendship should be.

title: 子夜吴歌
author: 李白
content:
长安一片月
万户捣衣声
秋风吹不尽
总是玉关情
何日平胡虏
良人罢远征
translation:
Chang'an, one sheet of moonlight
Ten thousand households, the sound of pounding clothes
Autumn wind blows endlessly
Always, the feeling of the Jade Gate
When will we pacify the barbarians?
When will my husband stop his distant campaign?
annotation: Under the moon, ten thousand women pound clothes for soldiers at the frontier. The wind carries their worry. 'When will he come home?' — the question echoes across the city.
